{"author": null, "closed_at": null, "collaborating_teams": null, "collaborating_users": null, "created_at": null, "credits": [{"login": "yorukot", "type": "reporter"}], "credits_detailed": [{"state": "accepted", "type": "reporter", "user": {"avatar_url": "https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/107802416?v=4", "events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/yorukot/events{/privacy}", "followers_url": "https://api.github.com/users/yorukot/followers", "following_url": "https://api.github.com/users/yorukot/following{/other_user}", "gists_url": "https://api.github.com/users/yorukot/gists{/gist_id}", "gravatar_id": "", "html_url": "https://github.com/yorukot", "id": 107802416, "login": "yorukot", "node_id": "U_kgDOBmzvMA", "organizations_url": "https://api.github.com/users/yorukot/orgs", "received_events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/yorukot/received_events", "repos_url": "https://api.github.com/users/yorukot/repos", "site_admin": false, "starred_url": "https://api.github.com/users/yorukot/starred{/owner}{/repo}", "subscriptions_url": "https://api.github.com/users/yorukot/subscriptions", "type": "User", "url": "https://api.github.com/users/yorukot", "user_view_type": "public"}}], "cve_id": "CVE-2026-64647", "cvss": {"score": null, "vector_string": null}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": null, "vector_string": null}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 6.3, "vector_string": "CVSS:4.0/AV:N/AC:H/AT:P/PR:N/UI:N/VC:L/VI:L/VA:N/SC:N/SI:N/SA:N"}}, "cwe_ids": ["CWE-116"], "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-116", "name": "Improper Encoding or Escaping of Output"}], "description": "## Impact

A server-side `fetch` with a request body may return a cached **response** body from a different request to the same URL but different body. Confidential data in the `POST`'s **response** body would then leak to unauthorized requests. Though the request itself will not be deduped.

This is only an issue when receiving request bodies with a content type charset other than UTF-8. For example, the UTF-16 byte sequences for `삃삃` and `섄섄` in the request body would share the same cache.

## Workarounds

If you cannot upgrade, consider only making fetch requests with UTF-8 bodies (default in Next.js). Applications using Pages Router are not vulnerable.", "ghsa_id": "GHSA-4633-3j49-mh5q", "html_url": "https://github.com/vercel/next.js/security/advisories/GHSA-4633-3j49-mh5q", "identifiers": title: Cache confusion of response bodies for requests with bodies containing invalid UTF-8 byte sequences
